senses.definition: in a distance that is considerably small

examples.x: The grocery store is within striking distance, so we can walk there.
The finish line of the race is in striking distance; keep pushing!
Our office is within striking distance of the coffee shop, making it convenient for breaks.

senses.definition: a distance that is very short

examples.x: The park is just a stone's throw from our house, so we go there often.
The store is a stone's throw away, so we can easily pick up groceries.
The school is a stone's throw from our neighborhood, which is convenient for the kids.

senses.definition: used to refer to something that is very close to a particular person, place, or thing

examples.x: The grocery store is just around the corner from our house, so it's very convenient for shopping.
The park is around the corner, so we can easily take the kids there to play.
Our favorite café is just around the corner, perfect for grabbing a quick cup of coffee.
The library is around the corner from the office, making it easy to access for research.
The bus stop is just around the corner, so we won't have to walk far to catch the bus.
I always keep a pen and paper at hand in case I need to jot down any important notes during a meeting.
The tools you need for the repair are right at hand in the toolbox.
I always keep my phone at hand so that I can quickly access important information.
Make sure to have your ID at hand when you enter the building for security checks.
Having a first-aid kit at hand is crucial during emergencies.
In the crowded subway during rush hour, passengers stand cheek by jowl, pressed together tightly.
The houses in the historic part of town are built so close together that they are cheek by jowl, with barely any space between them.
At the music festival, fans gathered cheek by jowl in front of the stage to get a close view of the performers.
In the cozy little café, the tables are positioned cheek by jowl, allowing for an intimate dining experience.

senses.definition: from one location to another location
used when two or more participants in a race or competition are very close and have an equal chance of winning

examples.x: The two candidates in the election are running neck and neck in the polls, making it a tight race.
The soccer match was intense, with both teams playing neck and neck until the final minutes.
The companies are neck and neck in market share, each striving to gain an edge.
In the talent competition, the two singers were neck and neck, receiving the same high scores from the judges.
The chess match reached a point where both players were neck and neck, and it could have gone either way.

senses.definition: in a place that is very far from where people usually go to

examples.x: I have never been to this part of the city before; it's definitely off the beaten path.
Next weekend, we will be venturing off the beaten path to find some hidden waterfalls.
We decided to take a hike off the beaten route and explore the remote wilderness.
Their vacation took them off the beaten track, deep into the wilderness, where they encountered breathtaking natural beauty.

senses.definition: very near to one's home or current location

examples.x: Having a grocery store on my doorstep makes shopping for essentials so convenient.
We're fortunate to have a beautiful park right on our doorstep.
Living in the city means having cultural attractions on your doorstep.
The new job opportunity is practically on his doorstep; he won't have to commute far.
With a supportive community on her doorstep, she felt a strong sense of belonging.

senses.definition: used to refer to someone or something that is accessible and near to one

examples.x: Her trainer was on hand to give advice.
We have a large quantity of food on hand for the event.
The store keeps a variety of products on hand to meet customer demand.
Having extra materials on hand allows us to complete the project without delays.
